what_it_does: Stores a ledger of design feedback entries keyed by mockup identifiers, with fields for preferred variant, verdict, note, and comments.
observations: Mockups include a specimen entry, two variant entries (var-15-currentsolution, var-16-fullbleed), and a decision-system entry (decision-sys-explained)
Most entries have empty comments arrays and null/empty preferred, verdict, and note fields
Only 'decision-sys-explained' has non-null values: preferred='v2' and verdict='redo'
standards: The only populated feedback signal is 'decision-sys-explained' preferring 'v2' but requesting a 'redo'
The file uses a consistent per-mockup object schema
conventions: Per-mockup record shape with comments, preferred, verdict, note
concerns: Most feedback slots are empty, so the file is largely a placeholder
No rationale is captured for the 'redo' verdict or the 'v2' preference
notes: A sparse design-feedback ledger that currently records only one actionable verdict: 'decision-sys-explained' prefers v2 but needs a redo.
questions: Why is 'v2' preferred yet the verdict is 'redo'?
What do 'var-15-currentsolution' and 'var-16-fullbleed' refer to specifically?
Who provided the feedback and when for each mockup?
purpose_vs_actual: The file appears intended to capture design review decisions, but in practice it is mostly empty and only one entry contains a verdict.
intention_signals: It seems to be trying to create a structured, queryable record of feedback per mockup so design choices can be tracked and revisited.
contribution: A per-mockup feedback schema that a fused system could keep to preserve design-review signals such as preferred variants, verdicts, and notes.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON feedback ledger for design mockups, mostly empty except for one entry preferring v2 and marking it as redo.
intention_for_embedding: To record structured design feedback, preferences, and verdicts for individual mockup variants.
